LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS EUGENE CHAN, BIANNE CHAN, NYUGET BACH DU, STEVEN YANG, EVA CHUK, LY K TAC, DAVID NGHIEM, THO HOA TRAN, NHUT SAP TSAN AND LIN FU FUNG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER TO THE NEGLIGENCE AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSES OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CCP 425.15. FURTHER, THE NEGLIGENCE CLAIMS DESCRIBE INTENTIONAL MISCONDUCT, NOT NEGLIGENCE. IN ADDITION, THE NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS NOT A DISTINCT TORT. THE TORT IS NEGLIGENCE. SEE BURGESS V. SUP. CRT., (1992) 2 CAL.4TH 1064, 1072. THE COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER AS TO THE SLANDER CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE BY THIS DEMURRER WHETHER CIVIL CODE 47(C) APPLIES BECAUSE IT IS A CONDITIONAL PRIVILEGE. THE COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER TO THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT. THE COURT SUSTAINS WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE PLAINTIFF CANNOT ALLEGE JUSTIFIABLE RELIANCE. THE COURT SUSTAINS WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CONSPIRACY CLAIMS AND CLAIM FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. THE CONSPIRACY ALLEGATIONS SHOULD BE PLEADED AS A GENERAL CHARGING ALLEGATION, AND THE REQUEST FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF SHOULD BE ALLEGED AS A PRAYER FOR RELIEF. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION, 302, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AND DENIED IN PART. THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST PLAINTIFFS PING CHING WONG AND MICHAEL HOU ON THE ASSAULT CAUSE OF ACTION. ONLY PLAINTIFF MS. MU LING LAI CAN MAINTAIN THIS CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANT LY K.TAC. THEY ARE THE ONLY TORTFEASOR AND VICTIM ON THE ASSAULT CLAIM. THEREFORE, THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IN FAVOR OF ALL OF THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS SAVE LY K. TAC ON THE ASSAULT CLAIM. THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE SLANDER CAUSE OF ACTION IN FAVOR OF ALL OF THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS SAVE DEFENDANT EUGENE CHAN. HE IS THE ONLY DEFENDANT THAT UTTERED THE ALLEGEDLY SLANDEROUS REMARK. THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IN FAVOR OF ALL OF THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS ON THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO HIGHLIGHT EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT. PLAINTIFFS' REMEDY IS TO PURSUE ITS OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO PROVIDE AN OPPOSING SEPARATE STATEMENT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(B)(3) AND THAT OMISSION IS ALSO A BASIS FOR THIS RULING. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (JOINDER), IS DENIED. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE COURT DENIED SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE ASSAULT AND SLANDER CAUSES OF ACTION. FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE, WHO INTERVENED ON BEHALF OF THE TSUNG TSIN ASSOCIATION, INC., FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE ASSOCIATION IS NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR MR. TAC'S AND MR. CHAN'S CONDUCT. MOVING PARTIES FAILED TO MEET THEIR BURDEN OF SETTING FORTH SPECIFIC FACTS SUPPORTING THE AMOUNT OF THE COST OF PROOF SANCTIONS THEY SEEK. (WEIL AND BROWN, CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL (THE RUTTER GROUP 2014) 8:1413.1 AND 8:1413.1A)). PARAGRAPH 9 OF MR. CHEN'S DECLARATION MAKES THE CONCLUSORY STATEMENT, WITHOUT ANY TIME RECORDS, THAT HE SPENT "160 HOURSIN DEALING WITH THIS LITIGATION, INCLUDINGTRIAL." NOT ONLY WAS NO TRIAL HELD IN THIS CASE, BUT MR. CHEN MADE NO EFFORT TO SHOW THE AMOUNT OF TIME HE SPENT "PROVING" THE MATTERS DENIED BY PLAINTIFFS AS OPPOSED TO TIME SPENT ON THE CASE GENERALLY. MOREOVER, A REVIEW OF THE SUCCESSFUL MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, WHICH IS THE PREDICATE FOR THIS MOTION, SHOWS THAT THE MOTION WAS VERY SIMPLE, RELYING ON ONLY SEVEN PURPORTED UNDISPUTED FACTS AND INVOLVING MINIMAL LEGAL RESEARCH. Paragraph 9 of Mr. Chen's declaration makes the conclusory statement, without any time records, that he spent "160 hours ' in dealing with this litigation, including ' trial." Not only was no trial held in this case, but Mr. Chen made no effort to show the amount of time he spent "proving" the matters denied by plaintiffs as opposed to time spent on the case generally. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS EUGENE CHAN, BIANNE CHAN, NYUGET BACH DU, STEVEN YANG, EVA CHUK, LY K TAC, DAVID NGHIEM, THO HOA TRAN, NHUT SAP TSAN AND LIN FU FUNG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - The court sustains the demurrer to the negligence and negligent infliction of emotional distress causes of action with leave to amend because plaintiff failed to comply with CCP 425.15. Further, the negligence claims describe intentional misconduct, not negligence. In addition, the negligent infliction of emotional distress is not a distinct tort. The tort is negligence. See Burgess v. Sup. Crt., (1992) 2 Cal.4th 1064, 1072. The court overrules the demurrer as to the slander cause of action. The Court cannot determine by this demurrer whether Civil Code 47(c) applies because it is a conditional privilege. The Court overrules the demurrer to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action because the Complaint sufficiently pleads outrageous conduct. The Court sustains without leave to amend as to the fraud cause of action because plaintiff cannot allege justifiable reliance. The Court sustains with leave to amend as to the conspiracy claims and claim for injunctive relief. The conspiracy allegations should be pleaded as a general charging allegation, and the request for injunctive relief should be alleged as a prayer for relief. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
